purpose: Defines the `!eager_dispatch [--dashboard {id}]` command — a standalone eager dispatch round that reads current state, identifies all dispatch-ready tasks, builds full production-quality worker prompts (with conventions, upstream results, reference code, KEY DETAILS), and dispatches them simultaneously. Use for stalled-swarm recovery, post-circuit-breaker resumption, or kicking off plan-approved swarms. Writes logs.json and master_state.json but does NOT monitor completions, retry failures, or run circuit breakers.
gotchas: Eager dispatch must include FULL worker prompts with UPSTREAM RESULTS, CONVENTIONS, REFERENCE CODE — not stubs (this is the key difference from !dispatch --ready)
Does NOT monitor worker completions — for full lifecycle recovery use !p_track_resume instead
Does NOT retry failed tasks (use !retry) and does NOT run the circuit breaker — it's a one-shot dispatch round
Includes an approval gate before dispatching — presents summary first
Reads project CLAUDE.md to build convention_map (whereas !dispatch --ready does not)
patterns: When-to-use decision matrix differentiates !eager_dispatch from !p_track_resume, !dispatch, and !retry
Differences-table contrasts !dispatch --ready vs !eager_dispatch on prompt quality, master_state writes, approval gate, and convention map building
7-action workflow: read state → build sets → identify ready → present summary → build full prompts → dispatch all → update logs+master_state
Rule of thumb: use !dispatch --ready for quick simple dispatches, !eager_dispatch for production-quality prompts
conventions: Two contrast tables (When to Use, Differences from !dispatch --ready) front-loaded
What This Does and What This Does NOT Do bulleted lists
Compact relative to other command docs — defers prompt construction details to agent/master/eager_dispatch.md and worker_prompts.md
